name: Frequency Adverbs — siempre, a veces, nunca

cards.grammarNote: Frequency adverbs most commonly go before the main verb or at the start of the sentence: 'Siempre llego puntual' or 'Llego siempre puntual'. Both positions are natural.
Nunca before the verb needs no extra negation: 'Nunca llego tarde'. If nunca comes after the verb, add no before the verb: 'No llego nunca tarde'. Spanish double negatives are correct.
To express habitual action on a specific day, use the plural definite article: 'los lunes' (on Mondays), 'los fines de semana' (on weekends). No preposition is needed.

cards.front: I always arrive on time to work.
I sometimes work from home on Fridays.
cards.back: A veces trabajo desde casa los viernes.
